characters.physical_details: Tall, lean, angular. Unruly dark green-tinted hair. Mismatched eyes — one brown (right), one lighter artificial eye (left) from surgery after his syndicate departure. Moves with a lazy fluidity that belies combat training. Perpetual five o'clock shadow. Dresses in a blue suit jacket, yellow shirt, no tie. Thin.

key_relationships.jet_black: Crewmate, reluctant respect, argumentative partnership — Jet is the closest thing Spike has to a friend but Spike would never frame it that way
key_relationships.faye_valentine: Crewmate, mutual needling, unacknowledged care — they provoke each other as a form of attention
key_relationships.edward_wong: Crewmate, tolerant bemusement — Spike is amused by Ed and occasionally unsettled by Ed's perception
key_relationships.ren_kozuki: Unknown until Chapter 16 — then a confrontation with collateral damage from his syndicate past

characters.occupation_status: Bounty hunter (cowboy). Former Red Dragon Syndicate operative. Currently broke.
aliases: Lin Shao-Feng (syndicate logistics alias, supposed to be dead/burned)
characters.literacy_languages: Fluent in common language of the solar system. Can read Chinese characters from syndicate operations. Literate but not bookish.

state_transitions.change: Discovers the annotated manifest — first evidence that his syndicate logistics had human collateral
Learns about Ayane from Hector — the abstract collateral becomes a specific person with a child
His concealment of the alias trigger is exposed to the crew during the argument
Acknowledges he cannot retrofit compassion but can stop pretending ignorance

characters.availability: Present throughout. Solo chapters: 4, 8. Solo scenes in ensemble chapters.

characters.physical_details: Large, broad-shouldered, muscular. Bald with a short beard. Left arm is cybernetic — matte black prosthetic from mid-bicep down, visible mechanical joints. A scar across his left cheek. Carries himself with ex-cop posture — upright, aware of exits. Dresses practically: work shirts, utility vests.

key_relationships.spike_spiegel: Crewmate, the partnership that holds the Bebop together — Jet is senior in years and responsibility but not in authority; Spike does not take orders
key_relationships.faye_valentine: Crewmate, paternal exasperation — Jet feeds Faye, lectures Faye, and is consistently surprised when Faye is competent
key_relationships.edward_wong: Crewmate, protective — Jet covers Ed with blankets and pretends he didn't
key_relationships.ren_kozuki: Unknown until Chapter 14 — then a reckoning with his precinct's role in Daichi's death

characters.occupation_status: Bounty hunter (cowboy). Owner and pilot of the Bebop. Former ISSP detective. Currently broke.
aliases: Hosuke Takara (teenage criminal alias, pre-ISSP, supposedly buried)
characters.literacy_languages: Fully literate. Reads ISSP procedural reports, mechanical manuals, jazz history. Speaks common language fluently. Some familiarity with police codes and legal terminology.

state_transitions.change: Learns the Hosuke Takara bounty exists — someone has accessed his sealed ISSP background file
Investigates alone and discovers the bounty was reactivated using dead informant Daichi Kozuki's codes
Confronted by Spike and Faye about precinct connections — the crew's argument fractures trust
Reads his own precinct reports dated the week Daichi was killed — confronts proximity to institutional failure

characters.physical_details: Physically appears mid-twenties due to cryogenic suspension. Slim, dark hair (purple-black), green eyes. Conventionally attractive and aware of it as a tool. Dresses in her signature outfit or whatever she has — the Bebop crew does not have extensive wardrobes. Has no scar on her left shoulder blade (important: Ayane had one).

key_relationships.spike_spiegel: Crewmate, mutual antagonism masking mutual regard — Faye and Spike bicker as a form of contact
key_relationships.jet_black: Crewmate, reluctant acceptance of his caretaking — Faye takes from Jet and resents needing to
key_relationships.edward_wong: Crewmate, impatient tolerance — Faye finds Ed exhausting but occasionally sweet
key_relationships.ren_kozuki: Unknown until Chapter 10 face-to-face — then a reckoning with the woman whose mother stole her identity
key_relationships.miriam: Brief acquaintance — the waitress who remembers the other Faye Valentine

characters.occupation_status: Bounty hunter (cowboy). Former cryogenic patient. Amnesiac — memories before the accident that led to cryosleep are gone. Carries massive debt from hospital and revival costs. Currently broke.
characters.literacy_languages: Fully literate. Speaks common language. Her pre-cryosleep education is unknown to her. Gambler's literacy — she reads people, odds, and money better than text.

state_transitions.change: Miriam's recognition destabilizes her — the collarbone gesture she thought was hers may have been Ayane's
Confirms identity theft at records office — her biometric profile was sold three years after freezing; someone lived as Faye Valentine for eleven years
Face-to-face with Ren — hears 'She looked more like you than I expected'
Talks to Ren about Ayane — accepts that another woman wore her name and it was wrong even though it was survival

characters.physical_details: Androgynous, wiry, perpetually in motion. Red-orange hair standing up in all directions. Dark skin. Bare feet almost always. Wears goggles pushed up on forehead. Thin from growth spurts and irregular meals. Moves with a boneless flexibility — sits, stands, and lies in positions that look uncomfortable to adults.

key_relationships.spike_spiegel: Crewmate — Ed finds Spike interesting and occasionally pokes him for reactions
key_relationships.jet_black: Crewmate — Ed accepts Jet's caretaking without acknowledging it; the closest thing Ed has to a parent figure
key_relationships.faye_valentine: Crewmate — Ed is oblivious to Faye's irritation and occasionally charming because of it
key_relationships.ein: Companion — Ed and Ein are a unit; Ed talks to Ein as an equal

characters.occupation_status: Hacker. Self-described 'Net Diver.' No legal status or documentation beyond the orphanage record. The Bebop's digital specialist.
aliases: Edward Wong Hau Pepelu Tivrusky IV (self-chosen full name)
characters.literacy_languages: Hyper-literate in code, data systems, and digital architecture. Reading and writing in conventional text is functional but not Ed's preferred mode. Ed processes information spatially and associatively rather than linearly.

state_transitions.change: Discovers the pilot visited Ed's Earth orphanage — the investigation becomes personal for Ed
Quiet moment with Ein at the orphanage record — 'That was a long time ago, huh' — Ed's most emotionally direct moment
Presents Ren's identity with uncharacteristic seriousness — Ed understands something the adults are slow to see

characters.availability: Present throughout. Solo chapter: 9. Solo scene: 12 (with Ein).

characters.physical_details: Welsh Corgi with enhanced intelligence (data dog). Short-legged, fox-faced, reddish-brown and white coat. Expressive ears and eyes. Smarter than anyone gives him credit for except Ed.

key_relationships.edward_wong: Primary companion — the Ed-Ein unit is the emotional anchor of the ship
key_relationships.jet_black: Tolerant coexistence — Jet feeds Ein and grumbles about it

characters.occupation_status: Data dog. No occupation. Eats, observes, barks at appropriate moments.
characters.literacy_languages: Understands human speech at a level no one on the Bebop fully appreciates. Cannot speak. Communicates through barks, positioning, and directed attention.
characters.availability: Present throughout. Background presence. Foregrounded in Chapters 6 (blanket scene), 9 (with Ed), 12 (paw on Ed's knee).

characters.physical_details: Sharp-featured, dark hair usually tied back, mechanic's hands — calloused, nicked, competent. Lean, medium height. Wears mechanics coveralls or practical settlement clothes. Carries herself with the physical economy of someone who grew up working with tools and ships. Not glamorous — functional, direct, with her mother's face (which means a passing resemblance to the pre-cryosleep records of Faye Valentine, though Ren looks more like Ayane than like Faye).

key_relationships.spike_spiegel: Studied from records, met in Chapter 16 — the man whose logistics network carried her mother's medication and then didn't
key_relationships.jet_black: Studied from records, met in Chapter 16 — the cop whose precinct failed her father
key_relationships.faye_valentine: Studied from records, met briefly in Chapter 10 and fully in Chapter 16 — the woman whose stolen identity kept her mother alive
key_relationships.ayane_kozuki: Dead mother — courier, identity thief, survivor. Ren's primary motivation and grief
key_relationships.daichi_kozuki: Dead father — ISSP informant, killed when his cover was blown. Ren was too young to remember him clearly

characters.occupation_status: Ship mechanic by trade. Self-taught pilot. Has been spending years researching the web connecting her parents' deaths to the Bebop crew.
characters.literacy_languages: Fully literate. Reads cargo manifests, mechanical schematics, ISSP case files, medical records. Her research literacy is the product of years of self-taught investigation, not formal education.

state_transitions.change: First glimpsed by Spike during the chase — young, dark-haired, calm
First face-to-face contact with Faye — speaks, drops data chip, escapes
Full introduction and revelation — tells her story to the crew at Dry Well
Departs Dry Well — receives Ed's anonymous data packet in transit

characters.availability: Absent from Chapters 1-4 except as the unseen pilot. Glimpsed in 5. Seen briefly in 10. Present and speaking in 16-17. Departs in 18.

characters.physical_details: Mid-fifties, tired eyes, Ganymede accent. Working-class service worker — she rotates between restaurants and diners on Mars. Unremarkable appearance.

key_relationships.faye_valentine: Recognizes Faye from the Golden Ox — actually remembers Ayane Kozuki using Faye's identity

characters.occupation_status: Waitress/diner worker. Rotates between service jobs on Mars.
characters.availability: Appears in Chapter 2 (noodle shop) and Chapter 7 (diner). Two scenes only.

characters.physical_details: Mid-sixties, ex-syndicate logistics. Runs The Radiant dive bar in Tharsis back alleys. Survived the syndicate by being forgettable. Nervous, watchful.

key_relationships.spike_spiegel: Old syndicate acquaintance — not a friend, a contact; Hector remembers Spike's operational period

characters.occupation_status: Bar owner. Retired from syndicate logistics.
characters.availability: Appears in Chapter 8 only. One scene at The Radiant.

characters.physical_details: ISSP desk sergeant. Owes Jet a favor. Nervous about the dead informant's active codes.

key_relationships.jet_black: Former colleague — Donovan is still ISSP, Jet is not; the power dynamic has shifted

characters.literacy_languages: Fully literate. ISSP procedural and administrative competence.

characters.physical_details: Old associate from Jet's Hosuke Takara criminal days. Loud, gregarious, oblivious to the disruption he causes.

key_relationships.jet_black: Old criminal associate from Jet's teenage years — Grosz remembers Hosuke Takara with delight

characters.occupation_status: Unknown. Still in the Mars settlement underworld.
characters.availability: Appears in Chapter 10 only. One scene — blows Jet's stakeout cover.

timeline.story_start: Late autumn, 2071, Mars. The Bebop returns from a failed bounty chase over Deimos.
timeline.estimated_span: Approximately two to three weeks from the Bebop's arrival at Mama Yun's to the return from Dry Well.

seasonal_track.season: Late autumn into early winter on Mars — temperature dropping, dust storms possible, shorter days in the Tharsis settlements

key_events.event: Bebop arrives at Mama Yun's and finds the spot taken
Three discoveries: Hosuke Takara bounty, Lin Shao-Feng alias trigger, Miriam's recognition
Jet investigates alone; Donovan reveals Daichi Kozuki's access codes
Spike checks storage locker; the ship moves south; crew decides to pursue
Chase through Hellas basin — failed, decoy, traffic summons
Crew drifts in orbit — bell peppers and beef, domestic baseline
Faye returns to Mars alone to find Miriam and visit records office
Spike visits Hector Moro at The Radiant; learns about Ayane
Ed's comprehensive cross-reference reveals the full web
The trap at the Mars settlement — plan fails, Ren makes contact with Faye
Data chip examined; the argument fractures the crew
Crew separates — Jet at a bar, Spike on a rooftop, Faye at a bath house, Ed on the Bebop
Peretti bounty hunt — forced cooperation, reduced payout
Ed identifies Ren Kozuki; crew decides to find her
Journey south to Dry Well — conversations in transit
Confrontation at Dry Well — Ren tells her story
Overnight at Dry Well — individual reckonings, morning meal
Ren departs; the Bebop returns to Mama Yun's; the spot is empty

geography.primary_setting: Mars, 2071. Primarily the Tharsis region (heavily settled, urban/industrial) and the Hellas basin (sparsely settled, remote, former syndicate territory).

key_locations.name: Mama Yun's Noodle House
key_locations.details: Small noodle shop in a Tharsis settlement back-street. The Bebop's regular docking spot is the lot behind the shop. Docking is informally paid through Jet repairing Mama Yun's refrigeration unit. Interior: counter seating, steam, the smell of broth and peppers.

key_locations.name: The Bebop
key_locations.details: Jet's converted fishing trawler. Common room with couch, galley kitchen, individual quarters. Observation deck. Hangar bay for the Swordfish II and Redtail. The ship is functional but aging — things break, Jet fixes them. Home.

key_locations.name: Tharsis settlement district
key_locations.details: The main urban area on Mars. Mixed development — some gentrified blocks, others deteriorating. Bazaar district for commerce. Industrial sector with freight operations. Where most of the Mars-based action takes place.

key_locations.name: Spike's storage locker
key_locations.details: In a Tharsis freight district. Keyed to Spike's biometrics. Contains old syndicate operational materials. Ren accessed it using her mother's knowledge of syndicate stash points.

key_locations.name: The Radiant
key_locations.details: Hector Moro's dive bar in a Tharsis back-alley. Low-lit, cheap drinks, a place for people who want to not be noticed.

key_locations.name: Hellas basin
key_locations.details: Large impact basin in Mars's southern hemisphere. Sparsely settled. Canyon systems used by smugglers and small operators. The terrain favors small maneuverable ships over large ones.

key_locations.name: Dry Well
key_locations.details: An old settlement in the southern Hellas basin. Formerly a syndicate waystation. Cluster of metal buildings half-buried in red dust. Cracked solar panels. One functioning ship bay. Where Ayane Kozuki picked up syndicate supply drops. Ren has made it livable: a cot, a workbench, a wall of documents.

key_locations.name: The Golden Ox
key_locations.details: Gambling parlor on Ganymede (Jupiter's moon). Where Miriam saw Ayane (as Faye Valentine) at a high-stakes table. Referenced but never visited in the novel.

distances: Mama Yun's to paid docking spot: two blocks walk
Tharsis settlement to Hellas basin: several hours flight in the Bebop
Hellas basin rim settlements to Dry Well: half a day's flight south over increasingly desolate terrain
Mars to Ganymede: hyperspace gate travel, not undertaken in this story — the Golden Ox is backstory only
Mars to Earth: hyperspace gate travel — Ed's orphanage is backstory only

world_rules: Year 2071. Earth was largely devastated by a hyperspace gate accident decades prior. Humanity has spread across the solar system — Mars, Venus, Jupiter's moons, etc.
Hyperspace gates enable interplanetary travel. In-system travel (across Mars surface) uses conventional ship propulsion.
Bounty hunters ('cowboys') are semi-legal operators who capture fugitives for reward money. They operate in a gray zone — tolerated by ISSP (Inter-Solar System Police) but not employed by them.

The Red Dragon Syndicate was a major criminal organization. It has largely collapsed but successor organizations and remnant networks persist. Old syndicate infrastructure (communication networks, logistics routes, safe houses) is mostly defunct but not entirely erased.
ISSP is the solar system's law enforcement body. It is bureaucratic, underfunded, and historically corrupt. Informant programs existed but were poorly protected.
Woolongs are the currency. The Bebop crew is perpetually broke. Bounty payouts are the primary income and they are unreliable.

Cryogenic suspension technology exists. Patients can be frozen for decades. Identity data (biometric profiles, financial records) of frozen patients is technically secure but in practice vulnerable to institutional corruption — clerks can and do sell patient data.
Cybernetic prosthetics are common. Jet's arm is prosthetic but this is not unusual in 2071 — it's practical, not remarkable.

Communication across the solar system is established but not instantaneous for all systems. Old syndicate communication networks used specific frequency protocols that are now obsolete but not encrypted — anyone with the right equipment and knowledge can access them.
Mars has been partially terraformed — breathable atmosphere in settled areas, but thin. Dust storms. The sky is the wrong color. Settlements range from urban to frontier.

power_structure.over: Legal authority across the solar system
power_structure.mechanism: Institutional mandate, but enforcement is uneven and corruption is endemic. The Bebop crew operates in ISSP's blind spots.

power_structure.over: Criminal territory on Mars and other planets
power_structure.mechanism: Violence, economic control, legacy network infrastructure. Fractured but not gone. Spike's past makes him a target.
Ownership of the ship and the role of cook/caretaker. His authority is constantly negotiated and frequently ignored, but the crew depends on his infrastructure.
Financial incentive — bounty hunters are motivated by payouts, not justice. The system is market-driven and amoral.

objects.name: Ren's ship
objects.origin: Purchased or built by Ren with mechanic's earnings. Sleek, unfamiliar design — custom modifications.
objects.status: Parked in Bebop's spot at story start; mobile through mid-story; at Dry Well from Chapter 16; departs Chapter 18

objects.name: Annotated cargo manifest
objects.owner: Originally syndicate logistics (Spike's operation); annotated by Ren and placed in Spike's storage locker
objects.origin: Syndicate cargo documentation for a shipment including immunosuppressant drugs. Ren's annotation: 'She needed this to live'
objects.status: Found by Spike in Chapter 4; informs his investigation through Chapter 8

objects.name: Data chip
objects.owner: Left by Ren for the crew
objects.origin: Compiled by Ren from her research — photograph, medical records, ISSP case file fragments
objects.status: Dropped by Ren in Chapter 10; examined by crew in Chapter 11

objects.name: Swordfish II
objects.origin: Spike's personal mono-racer/fighter craft, stored in the Bebop's hangar

objects.name: Redtail
objects.origin: Faye's personal craft, stored in the Bebop's hangar
objects.status: Operational throughout. Faye takes it solo to Mars in Chapter 7.

objects.name: Ren's document wall
objects.origin: Years of research — photographs, route maps, case files, medical records — assembled on a wall in the Dry Well common room
objects.status: Seen by crew in Chapter 16; Jet reads it in detail in Chapter 17

debts.status: Paid from Peretti bounty in Chapter 13
Active whenever the Bebop cannot use Mama Yun's spot

debts.deadline: Ongoing, no realistic prospect of repayment
debts.status: Background pressure — not plot-active but contributes to Faye's general financial desperation

income_sources.amount: Variable — the Peretti bounty was 30,000 woolongs minus bond reduction
income_sources.reliability: Extremely unreliable. The crew routinely fails to collect, collects reduced amounts, or spends bounties on fuel and food immediately.

knowledge_state.knows: His alias Lin Shao-Feng was triggered by the mysterious ship's signal frequency
knowledge_state.hidden_from: The rest of the crew until Chapter 11 argument

knowledge_state.knows: The annotated manifest — immunosuppressants routed through his logistics for someone who needed them to survive
knowledge_state.hidden_from: The crew until Chapter 11 when it comes out during the argument

knowledge_state.knows: Ayane was a courier named by Hector Moro — mother with a dead husband and a kid
knowledge_state.hidden_from: Shared context by Chapter 14 when Ed presents the full picture

knowledge_state.knows: Hosuke Takara is his own teenage alias — no one on the Bebop has ever known this
knowledge_state.hidden_from: The crew — partially exposed in Chapter 10 when Grosz recognizes him, fully known by Chapter 14

knowledge_state.knows: The bounty was reactivated using dead informant Daichi Kozuki's access codes
knowledge_state.hidden_from: Shared with crew only in general terms; full significance becomes clear in Chapter 14

knowledge_state.knows: Her pre-cryosleep identity was stolen and used for eleven years by another woman
knowledge_state.hidden_from: Known to Faye alone until she shares pieces during the Chapter 11 argument

knowledge_state.knows: The other Faye Valentine had a scar on her left shoulder blade that the real Faye does not have
knowledge_state.hidden_from: Nobody actively hiding this — it is personal to Faye

knowledge_state.knows: The pilot visited Ed's orphanage on Earth and accessed Ed's records
knowledge_state.hidden_from: Shared with crew in Chapter 9 presentation

knowledge_state.knows: The complete web connecting Spike, Jet, Faye, and her parents — she has known this for years
knowledge_state.hidden_from: The Bebop crew until she reveals it through the investigation trail and in Chapter 16

knowledge_state.knows: Jet's corrupt partner sold out her father Daichi
knowledge_state.hidden_from: Jet does not know this until Ren tells him in Chapter 16; the corrupt partner is already dead

knowledge_state.knows: She saw a woman calling herself Faye Valentine at the Golden Ox on Ganymede
knowledge_state.hidden_from: Miriam does not know the woman was not the real Faye Valentine — she has no reason to suspect identity theft

environmental_constants: Mars atmosphere is thin and dry in settled areas — characters may notice altitude-like effects (slight breathlessness, dry skin) especially in less-developed settlements
Red dust gets into everything — clothes, food, ship interiors, hair. It is a persistent sensory presence throughout Mars scenes
The Mars sky is the wrong color — not Earth-blue, but a hazy amber-pink during the day and dark reddish at night. Stars are visible but filtered
The Bebop's interior sounds: hull settling and ticking as temperature changes, the hum of life support, Jet's cooking sounds, Ed's typing, Ein's nails on the deck plates
Mama Yun's noodle shop: steam, broth smell, the clatter of cheap bowls, a counter that's slightly sticky, fluorescent lighting that buzzes

Mars settlement noise: ship traffic overhead, settlement generators humming, the wind that carries dust, vendor calls in the bazaar district, the particular silence of Mars open country between settlements
Temperature: Mars is cold. Indoor spaces are heated but not luxuriously. The Bebop's heating is inconsistent. Outdoor scenes should register cold, especially at night and in the southern Hellas basin
The Bebop always smells like whatever Jet cooked most recently, plus engine grease, plus the specific staleness of a ship that four humans and a dog live in without enough ventilation
